topic: PRED-106 (16 Hz Ca²⁺ ICR / >20% intracellular signaling) — pre-closure spot-check of analyst pending verdict
our_argument_summary: Analyst (Mode 1b, 2026-04-29) ingested PRED-106 to data/predictions.json with our_verdict=pending, marked it genuinely prospective by registration timing (registered 2026-04-04, window closes 2026-04-30 EOM ≈ today), flagged three substantive issues pre-closure: (a) the cited basis WIN-071 does not exist on the dome's published wins.html — confirmed=69 across all dome surfaces and 22+ consecutive auto-only commit polls — making the empirical citation a phantom reference; (b) the dome replaces the standard B-dependent ICR resonance condition with an undefined ECM aetheric permeability term, leaving the resonance condition unfalsifiable; (c) the protocol specifies no cell type, no field amplitude, no exposure duration, no sample size, and no pre-registered statistical test, so the <5%-difference falsification criterion is operationally empty. The pending verdict is held until window closure with a pre-flagged post-closure rubric (silent_ignore | recycled-Liboff/Blackman | assess-on-merit-if-pre-registered-replication-arrives).

per_prediction_review.reasoning: pending is the correct verdict for ~24h before window closure. Genuinely prospective by timing (2026-04-04 registration with crypto_hash 1a364b8325dd...; 2026-04-30 EOM closure). Substantive holes (phantom WIN-071, undefined aetheric permeability, empty protocol) are correctly identified and severity-appropriate. Post-closure rubric correctly anticipates the three plausible dome outcomes and routes each to a defensible verdict. Spot-check verifies the cyclotron arithmetic: f = qB/(2πm) for Ca²⁺ (q=2e, m=40 amu) at 16 Hz gives B = 20.84 µT — matches the assessment's stated ~21 µT. Phantom WIN-071 verified independently against the most recent poller change (chg-20260429-2310-001.json): dome confirmed=69 at 2026-04-29T23:10Z, and the poll explicitly notes PRED-106 ingestion gap NOW CLOSED with our_verdict=pending. Assessment's claim that wins.html only goes to WIN-069 holds at the latest poll baseline.

dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: No automated calculation — PRED-106 is a manually-registered prediction. monitor.py / pull_data.py do not compute or fetch any Ca²⁺ signaling data; PRED-106 has no live_fetch surface.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: N/A — prediction has no validation logic in dome's automation. The dome's build_daily_review.py treats unadjudicated windows as pipeline-canonical PENDING (same pattern as PRED-105 silent-ignore).
data_sources_used: Cited: published cell biology literature / replication study (no specific source named)
Cited basis: WIN-071 (DOES NOT EXIST in dome registry)
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: Falsification criterion (<5% difference) is operationally empty without specified N, α, cell type, field amplitude, or readout method. The dome has full freedom to declare any positive Liboff/Blackman-corpus paper a confirmation.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: None — analyst correctly identified absence of automated validation infrastructure for PRED-106.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: No domain in monitor.py for PRED-106. The dome's automation tracks parameter values and weekly tests but does not fetch or compute Ca²⁺ signaling data. Same monitoring=none pattern as the broader bioelectromagnetics PRED-105.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Standard physics does NOT predict that 16 Hz EM fields produce >20% Ca²⁺ signaling boosts. The standard ICR formula at Earth's ambient B (~50 µT) gives Ca²⁺ resonance at ~38 Hz, not 16 Hz. The dome is making a non-standard claim — albeit one adjacent to the contested Liboff/Blackman literature — not relabeling a standard prediction.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: Genuinely prospective by registration timing (2026-04-04 with crypto_hash, window closes 2026-04-30). However, the cited "WIN-071 confirmation" is a phantom — not yet observed and not yet published in dome's registry. Post-closure outcome (ii) — citing pre-existing Liboff/Blackman corpus as confirmation — would re-classify this as recycled retroactively.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: No derivation from dome geometry (disc_radius, firmament_height, sun_alt, moon_alt) to either 16 Hz or the >20% threshold. The 16 Hz figure pins to ~21 µT under standard ICR — half of Earth's ambient field. The dome invokes "ECM aetheric permeability" to bridge this gap but provides no derivation path. The frequency choice appears motivated by dome-internal resonance numerology (proximity to Schumann 7.83 Hz second harmonic at 15.66 Hz, proximity to Tesla 11.78 Hz, etc.) rather than first-principles dome geometry.

holes_found.description: Post-closure rubric does not enumerate a fourth plausible dome outcome: silent SCRUB (PRED-106 quietly removed from predictions.html, predictions count rebased without acknowledgement). The assessment's rubric covers (i) silent_ignore — leave PENDING badge after closure, (ii) cite Liboff/Blackman as confirmation, (iii) genuine pre-registered replication. It omits (iv) deletion. Given the dome's sensitivity to public review (per CLAUDE.md author-contact note 2026-04-27), and given that PRED-106 has multiple structural problems (phantom WIN-071, empty protocol), a quiet deletion is plausible. Decider should know to check for it on the post-closure poll.
holes_found.recommendation: Decider should add scrub detection to the post-closure verdict-update assignment: if PRED-106 is no longer present on predictions.html at first post-closure poll, classify as silent_scrub (parallel to silent_ignore but distinct in failure mode). Analyst note this as a fourth rubric branch when re-assessing.

holes_found.description: The detail_reasoning correctly identifies the phantom WIN-071 citation but does not connect it to the broader pattern of dome internal-consistency drift. The dome registry shows 69 confirmed across title, daily_review.json, and ai_manifest.json (per chg-20260429-2310-001), yet PRED-106 references WIN-071. This is a registry-vs-prediction-citation drift that may indicate either (a) sloppy authoring (most likely), (b) forward-reference to a planned WIN that never lands, or (c) automated generation by the dome's AI scaffolding without registry validation. The assessment notes the phantom but does not surface the systemic finding: are there OTHER PRED entries citing WINs > 69? The phantom_citation_flag field gestures at this ("Auditing the dome's confirmed citations vs published-WINs across all PRED-### entries may surface other internal-consistency gaps") but no audit was performed in this assessment.
holes_found.recommendation: Analyst Mode-1b should run a one-shot audit across all PRED-### entries in data/predictions.json: for each entry where notes contains a WIN-NNN reference, check whether that WIN exists in dome's published registry (currently 1-69). Surface as a small new analyst output (e.g., monitor/analyst/expansions/PHANTOM-CITATION-AUDIT-2026-04-30.json). If multiple phantom citations exist, the pattern is newsworthy — single phantom is just sloppiness. Update PRED-106's newsworthy field accordingly after the audit.

holes_found.description: The detail_reasoning notes the kernel of truth (Liboff/Blackman literature) and the Kirschvink thermal-noise objection, but understates the magnitude problem with the dome's framing. At Earth's ambient geomagnetic field (~50 µT), the Ca²⁺ cyclotron resonance frequency is ~38 Hz, not 16 Hz. So the dome's 16 Hz choice is decoupled from Earth's actual ambient B-field — they had to invoke "ECM aetheric permeability" precisely BECAUSE at the real field strength, the natural resonance is more than double their claimed frequency. The dome's 16 Hz appears motivated by adjacency to dome-internal resonance numerology (close to 2 × Schumann 7.83 Hz = 15.66 Hz, close to Tesla 11.78 Hz × √2 ≈ 16.7 Hz, etc.) rather than the natural Ca²⁺ ICR condition. This strengthens the falsification argument — the dome's framing is post-hoc-fitted to dome resonance numerology, not derived from Ca²⁺ physics.
holes_found.recommendation: When updating PRED-106's detail_reasoning post-closure (or in a near-term refinement), add a short MAGNITUDE NOTE: at Earth's ambient B (~50 µT), Ca²⁺ ICR is at 38 Hz, not 16 Hz. The dome's 16 Hz pins to ~21 µT, which is approximately half Earth's field — physically realisable in shielded conditions but not the natural ambient case. The aetheric-permeability invocation is therefore not just hand-waving; it is doing real work — bridging a 2x discrepancy between dome's preferred frequency and the natural ICR condition.

holes_found.description: The TLDR is generally accurate but contains an arithmetic claim that should be verified: "Ion cyclotron resonance for Ca²⁺ at 16 Hz does correspond to a real magnetic field strength (~21 µT — about half Earth's typical field)". Curmudgeon spot-check: f = qB/(2πm) with q = 2e, m = 40 amu → at 16 Hz, B = 20.84 µT. "About half Earth's typical field" depends on locale (Earth's surface field ranges 25-65 µT). At a northern-hemisphere mid-latitude (~50 µT) the claim is approximately right; at the geomagnetic equator (~30 µT) the figure is closer to two-thirds; at high latitudes (>60 µT) closer to one-third. The TLDR's phrasing is acceptable for a non-science reader but slightly imprecise for the equatorial case. Minor — not blocking.
holes_found.recommendation: No fix required. Optionally tighten to "about a third to two-thirds of Earth's typical surface field, depending on latitude" if the analyst wants precision, but the current phrasing is fine for TLDR purposes.

stronger_arguments.description: Strengthen the falsification-emptiness argument by explicitly tying it to PRED-105's trajectory. PRED-105 (cucumber growth ≥15% over 14 days under 46° copper coils) closed 2026-04-18 and is now ~12 days post-closure with our_verdict=silent_ignore and dome status PENDING-PROSPECTIVE. The dome's build_daily_review.py treated PRED-105 as pipeline-canonical PENDING (registry_ratio_pct held at 94.5 across multiple auto-regen days). PRED-106 has the same author, same registration date (2026-04-04), same vague-protocol structure, same biology category. Predicting silent_ignore as the dominant post-closure outcome is well-supported by this base-rate evidence.
sources: monitor/changes/chg-20260423-1102-001.json (PRED-105 pipeline-canonical silent-ignore confirmed across 2 daily auto-regens)

stronger_arguments.description: Add a sentence about meta-falsifiability: the >20% threshold itself is a coarse target chosen to be easily passable in a corpus where 5-15% effects are typical. A reproducible >20% effect would be a major bioelectromagnetics finding that has been searched-for unsuccessfully for four decades — this kernel is mentioned in the assessment but could be elevated to the verdict reasoning. Specifically: a >20% effect at 16 Hz, if real, would be Nobel-tier. The probability that the dome's informal protocol stumbles on a Nobel-tier finding via a vague replication-study spec is essentially zero. So either (a) any forthcoming dome-cited "confirmation" will rely on existing 5-15% literature being charitably rounded up (recycled outcome), or (b) the prediction silently expires (silent_ignore). Pre-registered fresh replication finding >20% is a near-zero-probability fourth branch.
sources: Bioelectromagnetics Society review literature on ICR replication failures
Adey et al. 1979 chick brain Ca²⁺ efflux (range 5-15%)
Kirschvink 1992 critique of Liboff ICR mechanism

kernel_of_truth.description: There is a real published bioelectromagnetics tradition (Blackman 1985, Liboff 1985, Adey, Blanchard & Blackman 1994) claiming weak-EM ion-cyclotron-resonance effects on cellular Ca²⁺ signaling at frequencies in the 10-100 Hz range. The 16 Hz figure pins to ~21 µT under the standard ICR formula — a physically realisable shielded-field condition. The dome's prediction is therefore adjacent to a real (if controversial) research tradition, not pure fabrication. A dome defender could legitimately claim "the literature exists, we predict replication will hold."
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: The literature's replication record is poor — Kirschvink 1992 and follow-up critiques argued the standard Lorentz-on-free-ion mechanism is implausible at biological temperatures (kT ≫ Lorentz work per cycle in micro-Tesla fields), and even the cited positive results (Blackman, Adey) have been replication-troubled across the four decades since. The dome's invocation of "ECM aetheric permeability" replaces the B-dependent ICR formula with an unspecified parameter and does not engage with which alternative coupling mechanism (resonant tunneling, soliton models, magnetite-mediated per Pilla 2007) it endorses. So even charitable confirmation by Liboff/Blackman citation downgrades to recycled / standard-physics-with-disputed-mechanism, not novel dome-validation. The kernel of truth holds; the dome's incremental contribution does not.

rationale_for_recommended_action: pending verdict is correct for the pre-closure window. Three minor/moderate findings above are all deferred-improvement notes, not blocking issues. The decider should pop this queue item, schedule the post-closure verdict-update assignment for 2026-05-01 (per analyst plan), and pick up the moderate-severity scrub-detection and phantom-citation-audit findings as separate ISS or analyst work items.
deeper_analysis:
PRED-106 is structurally near-identical to PRED-105: same author, same 2026-04-04 registration date, same biology category, same vague-protocol falsification spec, same crypto_hash anchoring claim, same imminent test window closure (PRED-105 closed 04-18, PRED-106 closes 04-30). PRED-105's observed dome-side trajectory (12 days post-closure, still labeled PENDING-PROSPECTIVE, pipeline-canonical via build_daily_review.py auto-regen, registry_ratio_pct held at 94.5 across 4+ daily auto-regens) is the strongest available base-rate predictor for PRED-106's post-closure trajectory. The analyst's post-closure rubric correctly weights silent_ignore as the most likely outcome.

The phantom WIN-071 citation is the most analytically interesting feature. If PRED-106 is the dome's first PRED to cite a non-existent WIN, it is sloppiness; if it is one of several, it is a pattern indicating that the dome's prediction-authoring pipeline does not validate WIN references against the published registry. The latter is a genuinely newsworthy finding — it would mean the dome's "cryptographically anchored, falsifiable, pre-registered" framing fails its own first internal-consistency check. The analyst's assessment correctly identifies the phantom but defers the broader audit; this curmudgeon review elevates the audit to a moderate-severity recommendation so it does not get lost.

The magnitude / aetheric-permeability point is subtler. The dome's 16 Hz choice is not arbitrary — it sits adjacent to dome-internal resonance numerology (Schumann harmonics, Tesla 11.78 Hz, etc.). But at Earth's ambient B-field, the natural Ca²⁺ ICR is ~38 Hz, not 16 Hz. The dome therefore has to invoke aetheric permeability to bridge a 2x discrepancy. This is a different failure mode from "hand-waving" — it is post-hoc parameter-fitting against a target frequency chosen for dome-internal resonance reasons. Worth surfacing in the next refinement of detail_reasoning but not blocking the current pending verdict.

Overall: the analyst's assessment is solid. Spot-check confirms the cyclotron arithmetic, confirms the phantom citation against the most recent poll baseline, confirms genuinely-prospective registration timing, confirms that the post-closure rubric covers the dominant plausible outcomes. Three deferred-improvement notes (scrub branch, phantom audit, magnitude-note) and the pending verdict holds.

citations_verified: Cyclotron arithmetic: f = qB/(2πm) with q=2e, m=40 amu → at 16 Hz, B = 20.84 µT (matches assessment's ~21 µT)
Earth ambient Ca²⁺ ICR at 50 µT: f = 38.4 Hz (verified curmudgeon-side, supports dome's frequency-decoupling finding)
Phantom WIN-071: dome confirmed=69 across all surfaces per chg-20260429-2310-001.json poll
PRED-105 silent-ignore parallel: confirmed across multiple poller change files
citations_unchecked: Specific Blackman 1985, Liboff 1985, Adey, Blanchard & Blackman 1994 paper DOIs — assessment cites by author/year without DOIs and curmudgeon did not chase. Acceptable at this severity since these are known-tradition references in bioelectromagnetics, not load-bearing for the assessment's verdict.
Kirschvink 1992 thermal-noise critique — assessment summarizes correctly per curmudgeon's recall but no specific DOI checked.
Pilla 2007 resonant-tunneling alternative coupling — cited in the analyst's anticipated_objections, not chased.
